string.text:
The Military Armament Corporation Model 10 is a compact, select-fire, blowback-operated machine pistol developed by Gordon B. Ingram in 1964. This specific model is oddly adapted for 9x18mm caliber ammunition in a "MAC-18" version for the Zone needs. 
 Such an innacurate bullet-hose converted to cheaper 9x18mm rounds seems to make a lot of sense around these parts. 
 
 %c[ui_gray_3]ATTACHMENTS:
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] 9x18 compatible suppressor
Modified M3 "Grease Gun" magazine with a capacity of 32 rounds, compatible with the MAC-18.
Extended M3 "Grease Gun" magazine with a capacity of 50 rounds, compatible with the MAC-18.
Grease Gun magazine modified to feed 11.43x23 rounds, holding 24-rounds. It can be loaded in the MAC-10.
Extended Grease Gun magazine modified to feed 11.43x23 rounds, holding 35-rounds. It can be loaded in the MAC-10.
